_comment: Phase 12 mutation testing — see AGENTS.md §5.3 + docs/MUTATION_REPORT.md. Uses vitest.node.config.ts (Node env, the same config whose 100% line coverage gate we're auditing). `src/index.ts`, `src/env.ts`, and `src/routes/**` (Hono glue) are excluded per §5.2.
_ratchet_comment: Break threshold is the current measured mutation score (floored). Any PR that lowers it fails. To RAISE the baseline, close surviving mutants (see docs/MUTATION_REPORT.md top-gaps list), re-run `vp run mutation`, and update `break` here in the same PR. `low` is a +3 ratchet target (warns, does not fail). `high` is a +8 stretch goal. NOTE: passkey Phase A PR #841 initially lowered break 90→84 (auth-do.ts + room.ts gate additions). Post-rebase-onto-main hardening (auth-do.ts: 77.55%→89.54%, 87→41 survivors, see auth-do.node.test.ts) closed the auth-do.ts-specific gap, but the authoritative full-worker Stryker run against the rebased tree measures 88.72% combined — still 1.28pt below this 90 floor. `break` is kept at 90 (not silently lowered) per explicit instruction; the honest current run FAILS this threshold and that failure is the accurate signal. Remaining Phase B survivor clusters (full-worker run, mutate scope src/handlers/**, src/lib/**, src/room.ts, src/auth-do.ts, !src/lib/webauthn-ops.ts): room.ts 183 survivors (largest single cluster — much of it main's own pre-existing surface, not passkey-specific, e.g. #armAlarm/housekeeping-alarm boolean/conditional mutants, ObjectLiteral prefix-arg mutants on storage.list calls), xlsx-import.ts 46, room-create-limit.ts 30, rate-limit.ts 26, xlsx-build.ts 21, auth-session.ts 20, authorize.ts 14, snapshot-storage.ts 10, ws-upgrade.ts 9, sandstorm-access.ts 8, handlers/rooms.ts 7, csv-parse.ts 7, loadclipboard.ts 6, cron.ts 5, multi-sheet-import.ts 5, remainder single digits. auth-do.ts itself: 41 survivors remain (89.54%), concentrated per auth-do.node.test.ts's own commit message in 8 clusters (loginComplete cred-lookup, decodeSession parsing, armAlarm/alarm-sweep object-literal args, registerComplete short-circuits, base64/hex encode helpers — several confirmed mathematically equivalent, getSecret memo/generation, timingSafeEqual/hmacHex primitives — mostly equivalent boundary conditions, consumeChallenge). Phase B follow-up required before this floor can be honestly raised back to 90: harden room.ts's largest clusters first (alarm/housekeeping boolean-flip and conditional mutants are cheap, well-isolated targets), then work down the remaining list. Do not silently restore `break: 90` claiming pass without re-running Stryker and confirming ≥90 — the last authoritative measurement was 88.72%, exit code 1 (failure).
inPlace_comment: Skip the tsconfig-preprocessor (and its `import('typescript')`) — we only run Stryker in CI and git worktrees, both of which are disposable, so a mid-run crash cannot damage real sources.
